raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Names the load-bearing concept (a diagnostic framework for multi-agent communication failures) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.4": "Identifies one prior work (MAS research) and articulates the delta (the absence of a principled framework for diagnosing inter-agent communication failures).",
    "R1.7": "Flags one missing or wrong citation (no citation for existing work on agent debate breakdowns, tool-use error taxonomies, or coordination failure analyses).",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence.",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the output introduces an overclaim about being 'the first operational taxonomy' without explicit comparison to prior work).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (a diagnostic framework for multi-agent communication failures) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.4: Identifies one prior work (MAS research) and articulates the delta (the absence of a principled framework for diagnosing inter-agent communication failures).
rationale.R1.7: Flags one missing or wrong citation (no citation for existing work on agent debate breakdowns, tool-use error taxonomies, or coordination failure analyses).
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the output introduces an overclaim about being 'the first operational taxonomy' without explicit comparison to prior work).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
